CREATE SEQUENCE my_sequence_name START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1;
CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, sequence_value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, sequence_value) VALUES (1, nextval('my_sequence_name'));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, sequence_value) VALUES (2, nextval('my_sequence_name'));
SELECT t.id, t.sequence_value FROM test_table t WHERE t.sequence_value < nextval('my_sequence_name');
SELECT t1.id, t2.sequence_value FROM (SELECT 1 AS id UNION ALL SELECT 2) t1 JOIN (SELECT nextval('my_sequence_name') AS sequence_value) t2 ON t1.id = 1;
WITH seq AS (SELECT nextval('my_sequence_name') AS value) SELECT value FROM seq;
SELECT a.id, nextval('my_sequence_name') AS sequence_num FROM another_table a WHERE a.id = 100;
CREATE TABLE join_test (id INT, ref_id INT);
INSERT INTO join_test (id, ref_id) VALUES (1, nextval('my_sequence_name')), (2, nextval('my_sequence_name'));
SELECT jt.id, jt.ref_id FROM join_test jt WHERE jt.ref_id < (SELECT nextval('my_sequence_name'));
